title: [perf] Golub-Kahan reduction: communication-avoiding micro-panel trailing update
description: Follow-up after frankenscipy-8l8r1.50 kept exact-order column-slice Householder kernels. Same-worker fmd evidence: public lstsq/512x256 improved 59.522 ms -> 48.483 ms and pinv/512x256 improved 71.677 ms -> 69.498 ms; public golden SHA stayed 1cdd3658c6caef8dec9fc58fa7e12b8d5c90151e2f93df91ffe2fcf862c16225; reduction digest stayed 0x90cdd3f8f71ed2c1. Reprofile still points at Golub-Kahan reduction as largest measured phase: 1024x512 reduction after keep 178.308945 ms vs bidiagonal SVD backend 52.554489 ms. Next lever must be structurally different from final materialization/replay/indexing: communication-avoiding micro-panel trailing update or equivalent deeper reduction primitive. Baseline via RCH on fmd or same worker first, preserve exact scalar-equivalence digest and public golden SHA, one lever only, keep only Score>=2.0, target at least 1.15x on reduction phase.

dependencies.title: [perf][no-gaps] Close ALL vs-upstream perf gaps in pure safe Rust — no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA linkage
